normalization_point: Low PBR is Stage 1; Green needs ROE/PBR, CET1, credit cost, and executed return together.
Bank holding value-up is Green-capable only when ROE/PBR, CET1, credit cost, and executed return all improve.
PF credit cost is a hard gate: low PBR and value-up cannot become Green while credit cost is rising.
Insurance Green needs CSM quality, K-ICS, loss ratio, and repeatable return policy, not yield alone.
Insurance capital release can become value-up only when CSM/K-ICS remain stable and actual returns execute.
Brokerage remains cycle/watch unless trading value, IB, PF risk, and ROE structure all support durability.
Value-up is Green only when policy becomes executed cancellation, dividends, ROE, and governance improvement.
Treasury-share cancellation is stronger than buyback news, but Green still needs business EPS/FCF and ROE/PBR confirmation.
Treasury-cancel mandate is macro tailwind; individual Green waits for actual cancellation and ROE/PBR change.
Completed cancellation is Stage 2 evidence, but business EPS/FCF failure can still block or break the thesis.
Holding rerating needs NAV discount plus actual governance and capital-allocation execution.
Tender offers and governance battles are event premium first, not value-up execution.
Payment fintech needs transaction economics and FCF; user count is routing context, not Green evidence.
Superapp and IPO optionality are Stage 1/2 until take rate, FCF, credit loss, security, and regulation are visible.
KRW stablecoin is an option value until regulation, reserve, redemption, volume, and fee economics are visible.
Digital-asset Green is blocked until regulated volume, fee model, reserve, and convertibility are proven.
Regulated stablecoin can become infrastructure, but only after reserve, redemption, volume, and fees are proven.
AI-agent payment is an option until fee revenue, compliance, and settlement reliability are visible.
Regulated stablecoin can still fail economics if user caps or unremunerated reserves compress issuer margins.
Stablecoin bank-deposit disintermediation is a RedTeam gate for banks and stablecoin issuers.
Algorithmic stablecoin failure is a hard RedTeam gate.
Credit-data infra needs recurring contracts and clean privacy/regulatory record.
VC/exit market is cycle-heavy and stays RedTeam-first until realized exits and cash returns are visible.
Fintech IPO optionality is Watch/Red until post-listing economics, take rate, FCF, credit loss, and security are proven.
Exchange consolidation can route research, but abnormal withdrawals or security incidents put RedTeam first.
Bank exchange stakes are Stage 1/2 until equity-method income, collaboration revenue, regulation, and security are proven.
Exchange market share is not enough; security incidents are hard RedTeam gates for exchange and bank-stake exposure.
Tax policy shock is a RedTeam overlay for brokerage, value-up, and crowded market rallies.
AI windfall/citizen-dividend policy shock is a RedTeam overlay for crowded AI, value-up, and brokerage rallies.
Governance execution failure is a RedTeam overlay, not a positive score bucket.
Stablecoin convertibility is a RedTeam overlay; issuance news is not regulated fee revenue.
Financial, capital-return, and stablecoin Stage 3 is capped when detail fields are missing.
